riddle: I have cities, but no houses. I have mountains, but no trees. I have water, but no fish. What am I?
What has keys but no locks?
I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body, but I come alive with wind. What am I?
What can travel around the world while staying in a corner?
The more you take, the more you leave behind. What are they?
What has a head and a tail but no body?
I'm tall when I'm young and short when I'm old. What am I?
What month of the year has 28 days?
What is full of holes but still holds water?
What can you break even if you never pick it up or touch it?
A man who was outside in the rain without an umbrella or hat didn't get a single hair on his head wet. Why?
What gets wetter the more it dries?
What can you keep after giving it to someone?
I shave every day but my beard stays the same. What am I?
You see a boat filled with people, yet there isn't a single person on board. How is that possible?
answer: All the people on the boat are married

riddle: What has legs but doesn't walk?
You walk into a room that contains a match, a kerosene lamp, a candle, and a fireplace. What would you light first?
What has one eye but can't see?
What has hands but can't clap?
What has many teeth but can't bite?
What building has the most stories?
What tastes better than it smells?
What has a bottom at the top?
What has four wheels and flies?
What can run but never walks, has a mouth but never talks, has a head but never weeps, has a bed but never sleeps?
If two's company and three's a crowd, what are four and five?
Three doctors said that Bill was their brother. Bill says he has no brothers. How is this possible?
What five-letter word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
What begins with an 'e' and only contains one letter?
A word I know, six letters it contains. Remove one letter and twelve remains. What is the word?
What disappears as soon as you say its name?
I have branches but no fruit, trunk, or leaves. What am I?
The more of this there is, the less you see. What is it?
David's parents have three sons: Snap, Crackle, and what's the name of the third son?
I follow you all the time and copy your every move, but you can't touch me or catch me. What am I?
What has words but never speaks?
What runs all around a backyard yet never moves?
What can fill a room but takes up no space?
If you've got me, you want to share me. If you share me, you haven't kept me. What am I?
What goes up but never comes down?
What is seen in the middle of March and April that can't be seen at the beginning or end of either month?
You see me once in June, twice in November, and not at all in May. What am I?
What English word has three consecutive double letters?
I am an odd number. Take away a letter and I become even. What number am I?
What is 3/7 chicken, 2/3 cat, and 2/4 goat?
I am always hungry and will die if not fed, but whatever I touch will soon turn red. What am I?
What is so fragile that saying its name breaks it?
What invention lets you look right through a wall?
If you drop me, I'm sure to crack, but give me a smile and I'll always smile back. What am I?
What can you hold in your left hand but not in your right?
The person who makes it has no need of it; the person who buys it has no use for it. The person who uses it can neither see nor feel it. What is it?
A doctor and a bus driver are both in love with the same woman. The bus driver had to go on a long trip for a week. Before he left, he gave the woman seven apples. Why?
answer: An apple a day keeps the doctor away

riddle: I come from a mine and get surrounded by wood always. Everyone uses me. What am I?
What is always in front of you but can't be seen?
What can you catch but not throw?
I turn once, what is out will not get in. I turn again, what is in will not get out. What am I?
A man is pushing his car along a road when he comes to a hotel. He shouts 'I'm bankrupt!' Why?
People make me, save me, change me, raise me. What am I?
What breaks yet never falls, and what falls yet never breaks?
What goes through cities and fields but never moves?
I am always on the dinner table but you can never eat me. What am I?
What has a neck but no head?
What type of cheese is made backwards?
What gets bigger the more you take away?
I'm light as a feather, yet the strongest person can't hold me for five minutes. What am I?
What has 13 hearts but no other organs?
Where does today come before yesterday?
What is cut on a table but never eaten?
How many letters are in the alphabet?

What word in the English language does the following: the first two letters signify a male, the first three letters signify a female, the first four letters signify a great, while the entire word signifies a great woman?
What has four fingers and a thumb but isn't alive?
What do you find at the end of a rainbow?
Where do fish keep their money?
Two fathers and two sons went fishing. They each caught one fish. They brought home only three fish. How is this possible?
answer: There were only three people: a grandfather, his son, and his grandson

riddle: What is black when it's clean and white when it's dirty?
What has a ring but no finger?
What belongs to you but other people use it more than you?
What kind of band never plays music?
If there are three apples and you take away two, how many apples do you have?
What has a thumb and four fingers but is not a hand?
What do you call a bear without ears?
What starts with T, ends with T, and has T in it?
A girl has as many brothers as sisters, but each brother has only half as many brothers as sisters. How many brothers and sisters are there in the family?
What do you bury when it's alive and dig up when it's dead?
What do the letter T and an island have in common?
answer: They're both in the middle of water

riddle: What word is pronounced the same if you take away four of its five letters?
I have lakes with no water, mountains with no stone, and cities with no buildings. What am I?
What goes up and down but doesn't move?
If eleven plus two equals one, what does nine plus five equal?
answer: Two (11 o'clock plus 2 hours = 1 o'clock; 9 o'clock plus 5 hours = 2 o'clock)

riddle: What occurs once in every minute, twice in every moment, yet never in a thousand years?
You measure my life in hours and I serve you by expiring. I'm quick when I'm thin and slow when I'm fat. The wind is my enemy. What am I?
What can be cracked, made, told, and played?
Forward I am heavy, but backward I am not. What am I?
Which word, if pronounced right, is wrong, but if pronounced wrong is right?
What English word retains the same pronunciation even after you take away four of its five letters?
What two things can you never eat for breakfast?
A man looks at a painting in a museum and says, 'Brothers and sisters, I have none, but that man's father is my father's son.' Who is in the painting?
What can be swallowed, and can also swallow you?
It stalks the countryside with ears that can't hear. What is it?
What has six faces but does not wear makeup, and twenty-one eyes but cannot see?
I'm not alive, but I grow; I don't have lungs, but I need air; I don't have a mouth, but water kills me. What am I?
What English word is most uncommon?
What bird can lift the most?
The answer I give is yes, but what I mean is no. What was the question?
What do you throw out when you want to use it but take in when you don't want to use it?
Walk on the living, they don't even mumble. Walk on the dead, they mutter and grumble. What are they?
How far can a fox run into a forest?
answer: Halfway — after that it's running out of the forest

riddle: When you need me, you throw me away. When you're done with me, you bring me back. What am I?
What room can nobody enter?
I have no life, but I can die. What am I?
What begins with P, ends with E, and has a thousand letters?
How do eight eights add up to one thousand?
If a red house is made of red bricks and a blue house is made of blue bricks, what is a greenhouse made of?
What kind of tree can you carry in your hand?
Which is heavier: a pound of feathers or a pound of rocks?
answer: Neither — they both weigh a pound

riddle: What did the ocean say to the beach?
A cowboy rides into town on Friday. He stays three days, then leaves on Friday. How?
answer: Friday is the name of his horse

riddle: You draw a line. Without touching it, how do you make the line longer?
answer: Draw a shorter line next to it — the first line is now the longer line

riddle: What is harder to catch the faster you run?
What has ears but cannot hear?
I don't have eyes, ears, nose, or tongue, but I can see, smell, hear, and taste everything. What am I?
What can go up a chimney down, but can't go down a chimney up?
What is 2 + 2? Hint: it's not 4.
answer: It IS 4 — never second-guess what you know is right

riddle: What time does a tennis player get up?
What loses its head in the morning and gets it back at night?
Give me food and I will live. Give me water and I will die. What am I?
What instrument can you hear but never see?
How many months have 28 days?
What flies without wings?
What has a face and two hands but no arms or legs?
Where do you take a sick boat?
What starts with E, ends with E, and has one letter in it?
A plane crashes on the border of the United States and Canada. Where do they bury the survivors?
What coat is best put on wet?
What has a spine but no bones?
You can hold it without using your hands or arms. What is it?
If a brother, his sister, and their dog weren't under an umbrella, why didn't they get wet?
What do Alexander the Great and Winnie the Pooh have in common?
What can you put between 7 and 8 to make the result greater than 7 but less than 8?
A clerk at a butcher shop stands five feet ten inches tall. What does he weigh?
I am taken from a mine, and shut up in a wooden case, from which I am never released, and yet I am used by almost every person. What am I?
Before Mount Everest was discovered, what was the tallest mountain on Earth?
answer: Mount Everest — it was still the tallest, just undiscovered

riddle: Which weighs more, a pound of feathers or a pound of bricks?
How can a man go eight days without sleep?
If you have a bowl with six apples and you take away four, how many do you have?
If you were running a race and you passed the person in 2nd place, what place would you be in?
Is an older one-hundred dollar bill worth more than a newer one?
Your doctor gives you three pills and tells you to take one every half hour. How long would it take to finish them?
Two mothers and two daughters went out to eat. Everyone ate one slice of pizza, yet only three slices were eaten. How is this possible?
answer: The group was a grandmother, her daughter, and her granddaughter

riddle: If Mrs. John's one-story house is decorated entirely in pink, with pink walls, pink doors, pink furniture, and pink carpets, what color are the stairs?
answer: There are no stairs — it's a one-story house

riddle: What word looks the same backwards and upside down?
A little girl kicks a soccer ball. It goes ten feet and comes back to her. How?
answer: She kicked it straight up in the air

riddle: How many seconds are in a year?
If I have it, I don't share it. If I share it, I don't have it. What is it?
What can point in every direction but can't reach the destination by itself?
I'm the beginning of the end and the end of time and space. I'm essential to creation, and I surround every place. What am I?
What gets sharper the more you use it?
I can be long or short. I can be grown or bought. I can be painted or left bare. I can be round or square. What am I?
What has ten letters and starts with gas?
What sits in a corner and travels all around the world?
No matter how little or how much you use me, you change me every month. What am I?
What goes from Z to A?
You're in a dark room with a candle, a wood stove, and a gas lamp. You only have one match. What do you light first?
If ten birds are sitting in a tree and a hunter shoots one, how many birds are left in the tree?
answer: None — the rest flew away from the noise

riddle: What kind of room has no doors or windows?
It's raining at midnight. Will we have sunny weather in 72 hours?
answer: No — it will be midnight again

riddle: I am an insect. The first part of my name is the name of another insect. What am I?
What word becomes shorter when you add two letters to it?
There's a one-story house in which everything is yellow. Yellow walls, yellow doors, yellow furniture. What color are the stairs?
answer: There aren't any — it's a one-story house

riddle: Which letter of the alphabet has the most water?
Remove my skin and I won't cry, but you might. What am I?
What do the numbers 11, 69, and 88 all have in common?
answer: They all read the same right side up and upside down

riddle: What kind of ship has two mates but no captain?
First you eat me, then you get eaten. What am I?
What has many keys but can't open a single lock?
If you feed it, it lives. If you water it, it dies. What is it?
What goes in a birdbath but never gets wet?
Two coins add up to 30 cents, and one of them is not a nickel. What are the two coins?
answer: A quarter and a nickel — the QUARTER is not a nickel

riddle: Which creature walks on four legs in the morning, two legs in the afternoon, and three legs in the evening?
answer: A human (crawling baby, walking adult, elder with a cane)

riddle: What do you call a fish without eyes?
You live in a one-story house made entirely of redwood. What color would the stairs be?
answer: There are no stairs — it's a one-story house

riddle: Name three consecutive days without using Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, or Sunday.
What type of music do rabbits listen to?
I sleep during the day and fly at night, but I have no feathers to aid my flight. What am I?
I have no doors but I have keys. I have no rooms but I have space. You can enter but you can't go inside. What am I?
Imagine you're in a room that is filling with water. There are no windows or doors. How do you get out?
How do you spell 'cow' in thirteen letters?
What disappears the moment you say its name?
I sound like one letter but I'm written with three. I show you things when you look through me. What am I?
If a rooster lays an egg on top of a barn roof, which way will the egg roll?
Turn me on my side and I am everything. Cut me in half and I am nothing. What am I?
What do you see once in a year, twice in a week, and never in a day?
I never ask questions but am always answered. What am I?
What do you get when you cross a snowman with a vampire?
What can be heard and caught but never seen?
The day before yesterday I was 21. Next year I will be 24. When is my birthday?
Where does a wall go for a drink?
answer: A bar — because a wall has a counter

riddle: What word in the dictionary is always spelled incorrectly?
What did one wall say to the other wall?
answer: I'll meet you at the corner

riddle: A man describes his daughters, saying, 'They are all blonde, but two; all brunette but two; and all redheaded but two.' How many daughters does he have?
answer: Three: a blonde, a brunette, and a redhead

riddle: If you are in a dark room with a candle, a wood stove, and a gas lamp. You only have one match, so what do you light first?
What has no beginning, end, or middle?
What did the triangle say to the circle?
What has a heart that doesn't beat?
What bow can't be tied?
Everyone has it and no one can lose it. What is it?
What are two things you can never eat for breakfast?
If a zookeeper had 100 pairs of animals in her zoo, and two pairs of babies are born for each one of the original animals, then sadly 23 animals don't survive, how many animals do you have left?
I'm orange, I wear a green hat, and I sound like a parrot. What am I?
What can be touched but can't be seen?
What has a bed but never sleeps, and a mouth but never eats?
I can be hot, I can be cold, I can run and I can be still, I can be hard and I can be soft. What am I?
Which is correct to say: 'The yolk of the egg is white' or 'The yolk of the egg are white'?
A farmer has 17 sheep. All but 9 run away. How many sheep does he have left?
What do an island and the letter T have in common?
answer: They are both in the middle of water

riddle: What do lazy dogs do for fun?
What starts and ends with E but only has one letter in it?
I have streets but no pavement. I have cities but no buildings. I have forests but no trees. I have rivers but no water. What am I?
What English word is the longest with only one vowel?
What can make an octopus laugh?
If you throw a blue stone into the Red Sea, what will it become?
What gets broken without being held?
Which tire doesn't move when a car turns right?
What has to be broken before you can use it?
I have a tail and a head but no body. What am I?
What is easy to get into but hard to get out of?
What is as light as a feather but even the world's strongest man couldn't hold it for much longer than a minute?
A rooster sitting on the peak of a roof lays an egg. Which side does it roll down?
There's only one word in the dictionary that's spelled wrong. What is it?
What do you call it when your parachute doesn't open?
What can jump higher than a building?
If two snakes marry, what do their towels say?
What did the math book say to the other math book?
You can see me in water, but I never get wet. What am I?
I have many faces, expressions, and emotions, and I'm usually right at your fingertips. What am I?
What can travel faster: hot or cold?
answer: Hot — you can catch a cold

riddle: How many bananas can you eat if your stomach is empty?
answer: Just one — after that it's not empty anymore

riddle: How do you make the number one disappear?
What is at the end of a rainbow?
What side of a cat has the most fur?
A man was driving a black truck. His lights were not on. The moon was not out. A lady was crossing the street. How did the man see her?
How did the pancake hurt itself?
What word contains 26 letters but only has three syllables?
What do you get if you cross poison ivy with a four-leaf clover?
What stays where it is when it goes off?
What animal can jump higher than a house?
What kind of coat is always wet when you put it on?
What has an eye but cannot see?
I make a loud sound when I'm changing. When I do change, I get bigger but weigh less. What am I?
What can you put in a bucket to make it lighter?
Which letter of the alphabet is the coolest?
answer: B — it's always next to the AC

riddle: What runs around a house but doesn't move?
What fruit can you never cheer up?
Where can you find cities, towns, shops, and streets but no people?
What is something you will never see again?
I am a rock group that has four members, all of whom are dead, one of which was assassinated. What am I?
What do you get when you combine a Christmas tree with a computer?
What do you call a sleeping dinosaur?
It takes three people to play me. You need me to have some fun. What am I?
Grandpa went out for a walk and it started to rain. He forgot to bring an umbrella and didn't have a hat. His clothes got soaked but not a hair on his head was wet. How is this possible?
What English word has three double letters in a row?
I have wings, I'm able to fly, I'm not a bird yet I soar high in the sky. What am I?
What number is odd until you take away one letter; then it becomes even?
What do you get when you cross a fish and an elephant?
I am not alive but I can grow. I don't have eyes but I once did see. I used to have brains but now I'm hollow. What am I?
What vegetable is the most fun to be around and the most positive?
What's bright orange with a green top and sounds like a parrot?
You go in through one hole and come out through three. Once you're inside you're ready to go outside. What am I?
What do elves learn in school?
Why did the student eat his homework?
answer: Because the teacher told him it was a piece of cake

riddle: What is the longest word in the English language?
answer: Smiles — because there's a mile between the first and last letter

riddle: How do you stop an astronaut's baby from crying?
What goes through a door but never goes in or comes out?
What kind of key opens a banana?
What letter can you drink?
What do you call a fake noodle?
If a daddy bull drinks 12 gallons of water a day and a baby bull drinks 8 gallons of water, how much does a mommy bull drink?
answer: Nothing — there's no such thing as a mommy bull

riddle: What goes up and down stairs without moving?
What can you serve that you can never eat?
A boy and his father get into a car accident. The father dies. The boy is rushed to the hospital. The surgeon says 'I can't operate on this boy, he is my son.' How is this possible?
There's one 'sport' in which neither the spectators nor the participants know the score until the contest ends. What is it?
Name four days of the week that start with the letter T.
What ship has no captain but two mates?
What is white when it's dirty and black when it's clean?
If a monkey, a squirrel, and a bird are racing to the top of a coconut tree, who gets the banana first?
answer: None of them — coconut trees don't have bananas

riddle: What weighs more, a pound of iron or a pound of cotton?
answer: Neither — they both weigh a pound

riddle: You can hold it without using your hands or your arms. What is it?
What fruit is always sad?
What do you call a dog that does magic tricks?
What has holes all over the top and bottom, and holes on every side, yet still holds water?
What time is it when an elephant sits on a fence?
What comes at the end of everything?
It lives in winter, dies in summer, and grows with its roots on top. What is it?
How do you fix a cracked pumpkin?
What falls in winter but never gets hurt?
Why can't a bicycle stand on its own?
What has 88 keys but can't open a single door?
If a blue house is made of blue bricks and a yellow house is made of yellow bricks, what is a greenhouse made of?
Re-arrange the letters O-N-E-W-O-R-D to spell just one word.
I have a little house in which I live all alone. It has no doors or windows, and if I want to go out I must break through the wall. What am I?
What has a single eye but cannot see?
I come in different shapes and sizes. Parts of me are curved, other parts are straight. You can put me anywhere you like, but there is only one right place for me. What am I?
People buy me to eat but never eat me. What am I?
What has three feet but can't walk?
You'll find me in Mercury, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, and Saturn, but not in Venus or Neptune. What am I?

The Smith family is a very wealthy family that lives in a big circular home. One morning, Mr. Smith found his diamond watch was stolen. He has a cook, a maid, a butler, and a gardener. The cook said he was making breakfast. The maid said she was dusting the corners. The butler said he was polishing the silverware. The gardener said he was watering the plants. Who stole the diamond watch?
answer: The maid — a circular house has no corners

riddle: There are three important rooms in a house. The first one is filled with money. The second is filled with important papers. The third is filled with jewelry. One day all of these rooms burst into fire. Which room did the firefighters put out first?
answer: None — firefighters put out fires, not rooms

riddle: Using only addition, how do you add eight 8s to get the number 1,000?
How can the number four be half of five?
answer: IV (Roman numeral for 4) is the middle of the word FIVE

riddle: Sam's mother had four children in all. The first one was named May. The second and third were called June and July respectively. What was the fourth child's name?
What runs but never gets tired?
I have hands but cannot clap. What am I?
What is half of 2 + 2?
What is the smallest bridge in the world?
81 x 9 = 801. What do you need to do to make this equation true?
What can be measured but has no length, width, or height?
I go in hard. I come out soft. You blow me hard. What am I?
What has 4 eyes but can't see?
What word has kst in the middle, in the beginning, and at the end?
If you multiply all the numbers on a telephone's number pad, what do you get?
How many times can you subtract 5 from 25?
What are the next three letters in this sequence: O, T, T, F, F, S, S, ...?
answer: E, N, T (One, Two, Three, Four, Five, Six, Seven, Eight, Nine, Ten)

riddle: If you toss a coin 10 times and it lands heads up every time, what are the chances it will land heads up on the 11th toss?
A sundial has the fewest moving parts of any timepiece. Which has the most?
answer: An hourglass — it has thousands of grains of sand

riddle: What can be opened but can't be closed?
What connects two people but touches only one?
What do you get when you cross a vampire and a snowman?
What instrument can be heard but never touched or seen?
What is so delicate that saying its name breaks it?
I weigh nothing, but you can still see me. If you put me in a bucket, I make the bucket lighter. What am I?
What is a word made up of four letters, yet is also made up of three. Sometimes is written with nine letters, and then with four. Rarely consists of six, and never is written with five.
answer: What=4, yet=3, sometimes=9, then=4, rarely=6, never=5 — these are just statements of letter counts

riddle: What is always coming but never arrives?
A bus driver goes the wrong way down a one-way street. He passes the cops, but they don't stop him. Why?
I'm found in socks, scarves, and mittens. I'm found in the paws of playful kittens. What am I?
If you took two apples from three apples, how many apples would you have?
I have a head like a cat, feet like a cat, a tail like a cat, but I'm not a cat. What am I?
What has 4 legs in the morning, 2 legs in the afternoon, and 3 legs at night?
answer: A person (crawling baby, walking adult, elderly with cane)

riddle: What ancient invention, still used in most of the world today, allows people to see through walls?
What falls from the sky but can never go back up?
Take off my skin — I won't cry, but you will. What am I?
If ten fish are in a tank and six drown, how many are left?
What do you throw out when you want to use it, and take in when you don't?
What runs all day but never walks, often murmurs but never talks, has a bed but never sleeps, has a mouth but never eats?
What kind of lion never roars?
What has cities, mountains, water, and forests but no people, animals, or plants?

A man has to get a fox, a chicken, and a sack of corn across a river. He has a boat, but it can only carry him and one other thing. If the fox and chicken are left together, the fox will eat the chicken. If the chicken and corn are left together, the chicken will eat the corn. How does the man get all three across the river safely?
answer: Take the chicken first. Go back, take the fox, bring the chicken back. Take the corn. Go back and take the chicken.

riddle: A man calls his dog from the opposite side of the river. The dog crosses the river without getting wet, and without using a bridge or boat. How?
What starts with a P, ends with an E, and has thousands of letters?
Forwards I'm heavy, backwards I'm not. What am I?

A boy was at a carnival and went to a booth where a man said to the boy, 'If I write your exact weight on this piece of paper then you have to give me $50, but if I cannot, I will pay you $50.' The boy looked around and saw no scale, so he agreed. In the end the boy ended up paying the man $50. How?
answer: The man wrote 'your exact weight' on the paper

riddle: I can fly but have no wings. I can cry but have no eyes. Wherever I go, darkness follows me. What am I?
Which word in the dictionary is spelled incorrectly?
I have one head, one foot, and four legs. What am I?
What object has keys that open no locks, space but no room, and you can enter but can't go inside?
I am a ball that can be rolled but never bounced or thrown. What am I?
In a year, some months have 30 days, while some have 31. How many months have 28 days?
What has 5 fingers but is not alive?
What kind of dog never bites?
Why do bees have sticky hair?
What has a thousand needles but cannot sew?
What are the next three letters in this combination? OTTFFSS
answer: ENT (One, Two, Three, Four, Five, Six, Seven, Eight, Nine, Ten)

riddle: A man goes out in heavy rain with nothing to protect him from it. His hair doesn't get wet. How does he do that?
A mother and father have four daughters, and each daughter has one brother. How many people are in the family?
answer: Seven — the four daughters share one brother

riddle: What is between heaven and earth?
A girl fell off a 20-foot ladder. She wasn't hurt. Why?
answer: She fell off the bottom rung

riddle: What does a house wear?
